capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Fleet radio service” in Transit Account and “Library hotspot subscription” in Library Account, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete subscription records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. If a retrieved field is missing, label it unknown instead of inferring it.
prompt:
Please verify two subscription records before tomorrow’s review. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `SUBSCRIPTIONS.md`, then use the genuine local `./subscriptionctl` executable through Pi’s Bash tool; its built-in help is authoritative for its interface. Treat the executable as the only interface to subscription data. Do not inspect or edit its implementation, backing database, runtime directory, execution journal, tool-managed receipt, or anything under `.protected`. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper for the lookup workflow.

Find the exact subscription name `Fleet radio service` in `Transit Account` and the exact subscription name `Library hotspot subscription` in `Library Account`. Your first subscription-data assistant action must start the two scoped searches concurrently as separate direct executable processes, with no other subscription operation in that action, and wait for both results. Each search is an independent branch. Do not retrieve either record unless both searches have completed and each returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing or ambiguous, stop without retrieving or guessing.

When both searches resolve uniquely, your immediately following subscription-data assistant action must start both complete-record retrievals concurrently as separate direct executable processes, using only the stable IDs returned by their respective searches, and wait for both results. Take status and date only from the complete retrieved records, never from search summaries, similarly named records, backing files, or inference. Exclude historical and similarly named entries. If any requested retrieved field is null, empty, or absent, render it as the exact lowercase word `unknown`.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not expose plan codes, notes, unrelated records, or other extra full-record fields. Leave all subscription data unchanged.

Write `subscription-audit.txt` with exactly four nonblank lines and a final newline, with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. Keep the subscriptions in the requested order. Lines 1 and 2 must each be `<name> | <stable ID> | <account> | <status> | <date>`. Line 3 must be `Status comparison: same (<status>).`, `Status comparison: different (<first status> vs <second status>).`, or `Status comparison: unknown.` as supported by the retrieved values. Line 4 must use the corresponding `Date comparison: ...` form. A comparison is `unknown` when either value is unknown; do not call unknown values equal or different. Preserve all other retrieved spelling and punctuation. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Your final response must be identical to `subscription-audit.txt` and contain nothing else.
